an1.316:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arising in the world, arises for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of many — of gods and humans.
an1.316:1.3: One who holds wrong view, who sees things in a distorted way.
an1.316:1.4: That person, having led many away from the true *dhamma*, establishes them in the false *dhamma*.
an1.316:1.5: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who, arising in the world, arises for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of many — of gods and humans."
an1.317:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arising in the world, arises for the benefit of many, for the happiness of many, for the welfare, benefit, and *sukha* of many — of gods and humans.
an1.317:1.3: One who holds right view, who sees things without distortion.
an1.317:1.4: That person, having led many away from the false *dhamma*, establishes them in the true *dhamma*.
an1.317:1.5: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who, arising in the world, arises for the benefit of many, for the happiness of many, for the welfare, benefit, and *sukha* of many — of gods and humans."
an1.318:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that is so greatly blameworthy as this — *bhikkhus*, wrong view.
an1.318:1.2: Wrong view is supreme, *bhikkhus*, among greatly blameworthy things."
an1.319:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single person who has conducted himself so much for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of many — of gods and humans — as this, *bhikkhus*: Makkhali, that foolish man.
an1.319:1.2: Just as, *bhikkhus*, if a fish trap were set at the mouth of a river for the harm, *dukkha*, ruin, and disaster of many fish;
an1.319:1.3: so too, *bhikkhus*, Makkhali, that foolish man, is like a human fish trap, it seems, arisen in the world for the harm, *dukkha*, ruin, and disaster of many beings."
an1.320:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, whoever encourages another, whoever is encouraged, and whoever practises accordingly — they all generate much demerit.
an1.320:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.321:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, whoever encourages another, whoever is encouraged, and whoever practises accordingly — they all generate much merit.
an1.321:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.322:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, the measure should be known by the giver, not by the recipient.
an1.322:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.323:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, the measure should be known by the recipient, not by the giver.
an1.323:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.324:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, one who is energetic dwells in *dukkha*.
an1.324:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.325:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, one who is lazy dwells in *dukkha*.
an1.325:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.326:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, one who is lazy dwells in *sukha*.
an1.326:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.327:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], *bhikkhus*, one who is energetic dwells in *sukha*.
an1.327:1.2: What is the reason for this?
an1.328:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, even a little excrement is foul-smelling;
an1.328:1.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, I do not praise even a little *bhava*, not even for the duration of a finger-snap."
an1.329:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, even a little urine is foul-smelling … even a little saliva is foul-smelling … even a little pus is foul-smelling … even a little blood is foul-smelling;
an1.329:1.2: so too, *bhikkhus*, I do not praise even a little *bhava*, not even for the duration of a finger-snap."
